distinctions between them can be made only by different functions
the four internal senses or subtle senses described herein

one is said to be situated under false ego
consciousness is the function of the soul

original substance, original matter is in the form of pradhana and in the pradhana there are
24 elements are there but they are dormant, they are not visible. And what are those 24?
That enumeration is given in these three verses. The first verse is talking about there are
five gross elements and they are known as bhū, āpa, agni, maruta, nabha. These are the names
of those gross elements and our body is made up of these mahābhūtāni, these five gross
subtle elements also, they are known as tanmātra, tanmātrāni. There are also five, smell, taste,
color, touch and sound. These are also matter only but they are subtle. And these tanmātrāni
are recognized as the subtle elements. These are known as tanmātra, tanmātrāni. There are
also five, smell, taste, color, touch and sound. These are also matter only but they are
recognized by or measured by indriyāṇī daśastrotrā. There are five indriyas for knowledge
acquisition. Here it is said indriyā means sense. Here it is mentioned the sense of
seeing. We have a sense of seeing, sense of smell and there is a sense of, it's called
A tactile sense, sense, we can recognize the touch and then there is a sense of taste and
a sense of hearing. There are five senses which, which can measure these five subtle
elements, tanmātrāni. And then also there are five working senses. A argan for speaking,

And then there are argan for working. Then traveling, generating and evacuating. These are the five senses. So, total there's ten senses, five knowledge-acquiring senses and five working senses. All are matter.
he thinks that these senses are mine, these are my senses, but actually these senses are
all matter and inside this body there is the spirit soul which is the living entity and
inside that living entity that soul has its own spiritual senses.
Soul is not Narakaar or Jyothi, it has its senses, soul has, soul is experiencing, it
has its own senses, but those senses are covered by this matter senses, senses made up of matter,
so this is twenty, five gross elements, five tanmatrani, ten, then ten senses, twenty, then
comes four, mano buddhi rahankara chittam, there are four internal senses, they are known as
mind, intelligence, ego and then chittam.
Chittam, here it is translated as mind.
Chittam is contaminated consciousness, all the four put together is called subtle body.
So these are the twenty-four elements which are potentially present in the pradhana and
So there is a difference between body and the senses.
Krishna explains in the third chapter of Bhagavad Gita.
That the senses are superior to body.
So body is made up of this Mahabhutani, earth, water, fire, air, ether and all that, but there
are something called senses by which we can recognize the sensations.
For example, eyes are there, it can see the color.
And it can also see how much color.
For example, something is read here, it perceives, there is a color it perceives.
This color is known as tanmatra.
for example, the sense of smelling is there, it can perceive smell, that is called tanmatra, smell is a tanmatra, but it can also perceive how much smelly it is,
obnoxious smell we say, that means something is very intense, so the senses can perceive these two, tanmatra and values, and so that's why they are superior to body,
sometimes senses can perceive means they can measure, they can identify, smell is there, it is a very dark red,
dark yellow or light blue, see we said light blue, blue is called color, that is tanmatra, less, more, this is values, tanmatra and value,
we continuously perceive this, all senses are like that, they are, they measure tanmatra and values, but they are not like,
sometimes they are compared to measuring instruments, for example, there is a measuring instrument for, say, recognizing sound,
decibel, there are so many parameters are defined, so, but actually senses are superior to this ordinary measuring instrument,
they have many superior abilities, the senses are like that, for example, I am speaking, the sound is coming, but the sensation, the sound is falling on the ears, but you may not hear also,
